paper_title: SIMS analysis of Si isotope for radiolarian test in Mesozoic bedded chert, Inuyama, central Japan

methods.description: This method describes the process of using Secondary Ion Mass Spectrometry (SIMS) to analyze silicon isotopes in radiolarian tests found in Mesozoic bedded chert from Inuyama, central Japan. The purpose is to understand the isotopic composition and its implications for geological studies.

steps.description: Collect radiolarian test samples from Mesozoic bedded chert in Inuyama, central Japan.
steps.searchable_summary: collect radiolarian test samples from specified geological location

steps.description: Prepare the samples for SIMS analysis by cleaning and mounting them on a sample holder.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for SIMS by cleaning and mounting

steps.description: Perform SIMS analysis to measure the silicon isotopic composition of the samples.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct SIMS analysis to measure silicon isotopes

steps.description: Calibrate the SIMS data using known standards to ensure accuracy.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate SIMS data with known standards

steps.description: Analyze the calibrated data to interpret the geological implications of the silicon isotopic composition.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze calibrated data for geological interpretation
